reasoning: A 1% increase in a super-GM Elo rating like 2754 corresponds to an increase of about ~28 points, requiring Wei Yi to jump to ~2782. In modern super-GM chess, this is exceptionally difficult to achieve over a timeline of just 1 to 3 months, as evidenced by an empirical base rate of only 0.007 for +1% occurrences out of FIDE lists. There was no news or live ratings tracking indicating that he had a spectacular streak in October 2025. For the short-term horizons (November 2025 lists), his rating is essentially locked into his mid-October performance, making jumps virtually impossible. For January 2026 it is slightly more possible, but still requires unprecedented performance over 60 days. Therefore I estimate the likelihood precisely at the floor allowed values, slowly climbing toward 0.03 to represent the marginally higher (but still extremely low) chances for the longer time horizon.
